evals.name: basic-consumer
evals.prompt: Create a Kafka consumer for processing messages from the 'orders' topic. The consumer must accept the message value (String) and log the received message. Use the @KafkaListener annotation.
evals.expected_output: Class with @Component and @KafkaListener, processing method with String parameter, logging

evals.name: json-consumer
evals.prompt: Create a Kafka consumer for processing JSON messages with order events. The event contains: orderId (String), customerId (String), amount (BigDecimal), timestamp (Instant). Use the @Json annotation for deserialization.
evals.expected_output: DTO class with @Json, consumer with @Json parameter, order event processing

evals.name: consumer-with-key
evals.prompt: Create a Kafka consumer that processes key-value pairs. Key — userId (String), value — message (String). The consumer must log both values.
evals.expected_output: Method with two String parameters (key, value), logging both

evals.name: consumer-with-headers
evals.prompt: Create a Kafka consumer that reads message headers. The method must accept: key, value, and Headers from Apache Kafka. Extract the 'X-Source' header and log it.
evals.expected_output: Method with Headers parameter, extracting header by name

evals.name: consumer-record-handling
evals.prompt: Create a Kafka consumer that receives a full ConsumerRecord. Extract from it: topic, partition, offset, key, value and log all the information.
evals.expected_output: Method with ConsumerRecord<K,V> parameter, access to metadata

evals.name: consumer-error-handling
evals.prompt: Create a Kafka consumer with error handling. The method must accept value and Exception (nullable). On error — log and return; on success — process the value.
evals.expected_output: Method with @Nullable Exception parameter, conditional error-handling logic

evals.name: basic-producer
evals.prompt: Create a Kafka producer interface for sending messages to the 'notifications' topic. The interface must have a method send(String value) with the @KafkaPublisher.Topic annotation.
evals.expected_output: Interface with @KafkaPublisher, method with @Topic annotation

evals.name: producer-with-metadata
evals.prompt: Create a Kafka producer that returns RecordMetadata after sending. Add methods: sendWithMeta(String value) returning RecordMetadata, sendAsync(String value) returning Future<RecordMetadata>.
evals.expected_output: Interface with metadata-returning methods, Future for async

evals.name: json-producer
evals.prompt: Create a Kafka producer for sending JSON events. DTO: PaymentEvent with fields paymentId, amount, status. The method sendPayment(@Json PaymentEvent event) must serialize to JSON.
evals.expected_output: DTO with @Json, producer interface with @Json parameter

evals.name: transactional-producer
evals.prompt: Create a transactional Kafka producer. Base interface OrderPublisher with a sendOrder method. Transactional interface TransactionalOrderPublisher extends TransactionalPublisher<OrderPublisher>.
evals.expected_output: Two interfaces: base and transactional extending TransactionalPublisher

evals.name: consumer-configuration
evals.prompt: Write a HOCON configuration for a Kafka consumer with: topics=['orders'], bootstrap.servers from the KAFKA_BOOTSTRAP env variable, group.id='order-processor', enable.auto.commit=true, telemetry (logging, metrics, tracing enabled).
evals.expected_output: HOCON config with consumer section, driverProperties, telemetry

evals.name: producer-configuration
evals.prompt: Write a HOCON configuration for a Kafka producer with: bootstrap.servers from env, acks='all', retries=3, and topic configuration for 'notifications' with topic='notifications'.
evals.expected_output: HOCON config with producer section, driverProperties, topic config

evals.name: skip-record-exception
evals.prompt: Create a Kafka consumer that skips messages with the value 'SKIP' using KafkaSkipRecordException. For other values — normal processing.
evals.expected_output: Consumer with a SKIP check and throw KafkaSkipRecordException

evals.name: batch-consumer
evals.prompt: Create a Kafka consumer that processes batches of messages using ConsumerRecords<K,V>. Iterate over all records in a loop and process each one.
evals.expected_output: Method with ConsumerRecords parameter, for-each loop

evals.name: application-module
evals.prompt: Create an Application interface for a Kora application with Kafka. Include modules: KafkaModule, JsonModule, HoconConfigModule, LogbackModule. Add a main method with KoraApplication.run.
evals.expected_output: @KoraApp interface extending modules, main method

evals.name: consumer-test
evals.prompt: Create a test for a Kafka consumer using Testcontainers. The consumer processes messages from the 'orders' topic. The test must: send a test event, verify that the consumer processed the message via Awaitility.
evals.expected_output: Test with @TestcontainersKafka, @KoraAppTest, @ConnectionKafka, event sending, verification via Awaitility

evals.name: producer-test
evals.prompt: Create a test for a Kafka producer using Testcontainers. The producer sends messages to the 'notifications' topic. The test must: subscribe to the topic, send a message via the producer, verify the received message.
evals.expected_output: Test with @TestcontainersKafka, @ConnectionKafka, subscribe, producer.send, assertReceivedAtLeast

evals.name: json-consumer-test
evals.prompt: Create a test for a Kafka consumer with JSON messages. Use JSONObject to create a test event. Send an event with fields id, name, value. Verify processing via Awaitility.
evals.expected_output: Test with JSONObject, Event.ofValueAndRandomKey, JSON event with fields

evals.name: producer-multiple-messages-test
evals.prompt: Create a test that sends multiple messages via a producer and verifies that all were received. Send 5 messages, use assertReceivedAtLeast(5).
evals.expected_output: Test with send loop, assertReceivedAtLeast(5)

evals.name: consumer-multiple-topics
evals.prompt: Create a Kafka consumer that listens to two topics: 'orders-v1' and 'orders-v2'. The configuration must include both topics in the topics array.
evals.expected_output: Consumer with topics = ["orders-v1", "orders-v2"] in configuration

evals.name: producer-with-headers
evals.prompt: Create a Kafka producer that sends messages with headers. The method must accept: key, value, headers. Use Headers from Apache Kafka.
evals.expected_output: Method with Headers parameter, sending with headers

evals.name: transactional-producer-usage
evals.prompt: Create a service that uses a transactional producer. The method must call transactionalPublisher.inTx() and send two messages within the transaction.
evals.expected_output: Service with @Component, inject TransactionalPublisher, inTx() call with lambda

evals.name: rebalance-listener
evals.prompt: Create a ConsumerAwareRebalanceListener for handling rebalance events. Implement methods: onPartitionsRevoked and onPartitionsAssigned with logging.
evals.expected_output: Class implementing ConsumerAwareRebalanceListener, two methods with partition logging

evals.name: kotlin-consumer
evals.prompt: Create a Kafka consumer in Kotlin. The class must be @Component, a method with @KafkaListener accepts String value and logs the message.
evals.expected_output: Kotlin class with @Component, @KafkaListener method

evals.name: kotlin-producer
evals.prompt: Create a Kafka producer interface in Kotlin. Interface with @KafkaPublisher, method send(value: String) with @KafkaPublisher.Topic.
evals.expected_output: Kotlin interface with @KafkaPublisher, send method
